title: US ROK Forces Formalize Joint Medical Training and Drone Integration

contents:
## US ROK Forces Formalize Joint Medical Training and Drone Integration

### Overview
The U.S. Army's 65th Medical Brigade and the Republic of Korea Army Noncommissioned Officer Academy signed a memorandum of agreement on April 27 to strengthen combat casualty care and interoperability. The partnership establishes recurring training exchanges focused on Tactical Combat Casualty Care, professional instructor development, and tactical drone employment.

### Event Details
The Republic of Korea Army Noncommissioned Officer Academy and the U.S. Army's 65th Medical Brigade formalized a bilateral partnership through the signing of a memorandum of agreement aimed at strengthening combat casualty care and preserving combat power across the alliance. The agreement establishes a recurring training exchange focused on Tactical Combat Casualty Care (TCCC), professional instructor development, and joint medical training designed to improve interoperability in wartime conditions. The partnership includes semiannual joint medical training events, with the first iteration conducted at the ROK Army NCO Academy and follow-on training hosted by the 65th Medical Brigade later in the year. These events focus on casualty evacuation procedures, battlefield trauma management, and integrated medical operations. Beyond medical training, the ROK Army provides instruction to U.S. personnel on tactical drone employment, including first-person view drone operations. This exchange reflects a broader effort to incorporate emerging battlefield technologies into combined training. The 65th Medical Brigade, the U.S. Army's only forward-deployed medical brigade, plays a critical role in providing medical support to U.S. Forces Korea while strengthening combined medical readiness with ROK partners. The agreement reinforces the enduring U.S.-ROK alliance and ensures both forces remain ready to preserve combat power and respond effectively in future conflicts.

### Source Context
@chadobcnews (Chad O'Brien) covers this category of developments. The account focuses on korea us forces korea defense military deployment rok breaking news.
